t_ch: 等我凯旋！
t_en: Wait for my triumphant return!

t_ch: 我会拍下决定性的一刻！
t_en: I'll capture all the decisive moments!

t_ch: 保护好自己，量力而行。
t_en: Keep yourself safe, and work within your capabilities.

t_ch: 咳咳…站稳啦，听好啦！
t_en: Ahem! Stand still and listen up!

t_ch: 我们，雅努萨波利斯的缇宝、缇安、缇宁，黄金裔的信使，承载{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}神权的半神——对你们施以祝福！
t_en: We — Tribbie, Trianne, and Trinnon from Janusopolis, messengers of the Chrysos Heirs and the demigods who bear {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}'s divine authority — give unto you our blessings!

t_ch: 愿命运向你们展现善意的面容，封锁每条通向死路的歧途……
t_en: May fate show you benevolence and seal away all paths that lead to dead ends...

t_ch: ——敞开每扇通向胜利的大门！
t_en: And throw open all gates that lead to victory!

t_ch: 还得再做做心理准备…
t_en: I still need a moment to mentally prepare...

t_ch: 准备，准备！等不及啦，好慢！
t_en: Get ready! Get ready! I don't wanna wait! Slowpoke!

t_ch: 抱歉啦，缇安老师。事关重大，再给我们多点耐心吧。
t_en: I'm sorry, Trianne. This is a matter of grave importance, so please bear with us.

t_ch: 我打算多花些时间留在这，向缇宝女士讨教翁法罗斯的知识。你负责行动，我负责研究，我们各司其职。
t_en: I plan to stay here for a while and learn more about Amphoreus from Lady Tribbie. You'll handle the action, and I'll deal with the research. We each have a role to play.

t_ch: 不来个临别的拥抱吗？
t_en: Shall we have a goodbye hug?

t_ch: 丹恒用沉默拒绝了你的提议。
t_en: Dan Heng refuses your proposal with silence.

t_ch: 请…以得胜的姿态归来。
t_en: Please, come back victorious.

t_ch: 我会为你祝祷，愿你永远比死亡的追逐行得更快。
t_en: I'll pray for you. May your steps be ever quicker than death's relentless pursuit.

t_ch: 神意使然，你终究还是站在了奥赫玛这边。
t_en: It is by the will of the gods that you stood on Okhema's side in the end.

t_ch: 不是不信任我么？
t_en: I thought you didn't trust me.

t_ch: 这和你没有关系。
t_en: No thanks to you.

t_ch: 你是白厄与万敌的同伴，只要他们愿意向你托付生死，我的信任并不重要。
t_en: You are Phainon and Mydei's companion. So long as they're willing to entrust you with their lives, my trust in you doesn't matter.

t_ch: 去吧，和两位黄金裔一起。奥赫玛需要一位真正的战神。疯狂的{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}，他的统治该被终结了。
t_en: Go. Stand with the two Chrysos Heirs. Okhema has need of a true god of war. It's time for the mad god {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador's{RUBY_E#} reign to end.

t_ch: 对不住，我应该更早些赶到的。请你们再听我说几句。
t_en: Sorry, I should've gotten here earlier. Please hear me out before you make up your mind.

t_ch: 聆听星宿的声音…这也能做到么？
t_en: Listening to the voices of the constellations... Is that really possible?

t_ch: 嗯…十二位泰坦的星宿都拍进去了。离开这里吧。
t_en: Alright... All the constellations of the twelve Titans are in there. Time to head out.

t_ch: 照白厄说的，去灵水盆前冥想吧。
t_en: Let's go meditate in front of the spirit basin like Phainon told us.

t_ch: 没想到，一觉刚醒就要踏上征程了。
t_en: I wasn't expecting the expedition to set off after only a night of rest.

t_ch: 先把问题放在一旁，去参加黄金裔的战前动员吧。
t_en: Let's set the questions aside for now. The Chrysos Heirs are getting ready to mobilize.

t_ch: 丹恒
t_en: Dan Heng

t_ch: 白厄
t_en: Phainon

t_ch: 遐蝶
t_en: Castorice

t_ch: 缇里西庇俄丝
t_en: Tribios

t_ch: 阿格莱雅
t_en: Aglaea

t_ch: 迈德漠斯
t_en: Mydeimos

t_ch: {RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}
t_en: {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}

t_ch: 遐蝶  ？？
t_en: Castorice?

t_ch: 缇宝 缇宁 缇安  ？？  ？？  ？？……
t_en: Tribbie, Trinnon, Trianne  ??  ??  ??...

t_ch: 阿格莱雅
t_en: Aglaea

t_ch: 万敌
t_en: Mydei

t_ch: {RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}
t_en: {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}

t_ch: 「永恒之地」翁法罗斯
t_en: Amphoreus, The Eternal Land

t_ch: 完
t_en: The End

t_ch: 《崩坏：星穹铁道》
t_en: Honkai: Star Rail

t_ch: 领衔主演
t_en: Lead Actor

t_ch: {NICKNAME}
t_en: {NICKNAME}

t_ch: 主演
t_en: Starring

t_ch: …比想象中安静得多啊。
t_en: It's quieter than I expected...

t_ch: 腐朽…但又令人熟悉。
t_en: It's corrupted... but still familiar.

t_ch: 这里就是「悬锋城」。
t_en: This is Castrum Kremnos.

t_ch: 没想到时移世易，连{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的腹地都在溃散边缘了，一个它的眷属都看不见。
t_en: Times really have changed. Even {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador's{RUBY_E#} own lands are on the verge of collapse. I haven't seen any sign of Titankin.

t_ch: 要是悬锋城还留有昔日兵力，哪怕只剩半成，阿格莱雅也是在让我们送死。
t_en: If even half of Castrum Kremnos' old might remains, it would mean that Aglaea sent us to our deaths.

t_ch: 这里是外城廊道，过去由重兵把守，即使你我联手——至多走出三十步距离。
t_en: This is the outer city corridor. It used to be heavily guarded by soldiers. Even with the two of us working together, we wouldn't have made it past thirty steps.

t_ch: 那加上我呢？
t_en: What if you added me to the count?

t_ch: 触景生情，想家了？
t_en: Is seeing all this making you homesick?

t_ch: 随你怎么想。
t_en: Think whatever you want.

t_ch: 是啊，悬锋城曾是每一位战士的向往之地。
t_en: Yeah, Kremnos used to be the place every warrior longed for.

t_ch: …万敌，你会觉得可惜吗？自己没有活在那个强者如云的年代。若非往日的英雄一一凋零，我也不会是你唯一的对手。
t_en: Kind of a shame you didn't live in that era full of strong warriors, eh Mydei? If all the heroes of the past hadn't fallen one after another, I wouldn't be the only worthy opponent you have left.

t_ch: 你害怕了？现在逃跑还来得及。
t_en: Are you scared? It's still not too late to run.

t_ch: 不，只是想起从前的勇士也是沿着这条廊道，向{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}「天谴之矛」{RUBY_E#}的城池进发，为自己的名字镀上金边。
t_en: No, I'm just thinking about the warriors who also braved this very corridor in the past. They all ventured towards the city of {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}the Lance of Fury{RUBY_E#}, all just to set their names in gold.

t_ch: 即便这座堡垒已然破败，但我们此刻要去做的事也没什么不同。
t_en: The fortress may be in ruins now, but what we're about to do is no different from what they were striving for back then.

t_ch: 走吧，夺回「纷争」的火种，或是死在它的悬锋下——
t_en: Let's go. We'll take back the Coreflame of Strife or die in Kremnos.

t_ch: 这两种荣耀，我们只能二选其一。
t_en: Those are the only two paths to glory we can choose from.

t_ch: 缇安老师，前面危险重重，你留在这里，找个安全的地方躲起来。
t_en: Trianne, the road ahead is dangerous. You should stay here and find a safe place to hide.

t_ch: 我们完成使命就会回来，到时再麻烦你使用「百界门」，好吗？
t_en: We'll return once we complete our mission. Then we'll have to trouble you to use the Century Gate again, alright?

t_ch: 明白啦。好好躲起来，不让坏人找到！
t_en: Got it. Hide so the baddies can't find me!

t_ch: 她真的是「半神」吗…
t_en: Is she really a demigod...

t_ch: 怎么看都像个小孩子。
t_en: She still looks and acts just like a child.

t_ch: 为了翁法罗斯，缇安老师也付出了很多代价。
t_en: Trianne has sacrificed a lot for Amphoreus.

t_ch: 说起来，你不打算给{NICKNAME}讲讲悬锋城的事吗？不担心我给{F#她}{M#他}灌输些你最痛恨的野史？
t_en: By the way, aren't you going to tell {NICKNAME} any stories about Castrum Kremnos? Aren't you worried I'll tell {M#him}{F#her} all the historical myths you despise?

t_ch: 你尽管试试。
t_en: I'd like to see you try.

t_ch: 唉，有时跟你说话真的累……
t_en: *sigh* It's exhausting talking to you sometimes...

t_ch: 给我说说这吧。
t_en: Tell me about this one.

t_ch: 这画的是俄罗斯方块吗？
t_en: Is this Tetris?

t_ch: 悬锋城自古便是{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的领地，是信众围绕泰坦建立的军事要塞，也是一座巨大的移动堡垒。
t_en: Castrum Kremnos has been {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}'s territory since ancient times. It's a massive moving fortress that surrounds the Titan, built by their followers.

t_ch: 在{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}「天谴之矛」{RUBY_E#}尚未堕落的年代，悬锋城会随它一同出征，以战养战，是纪律严明、极端尚武的城邦。
t_en: In the age before the {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Lance of Fury{RUBY_E#} fell from grace, Castrum Kremnos would follow the Lance into battle after battle. It's a city that enforces strict discipline and reveres military might.

t_ch: 这么一对比，我老家的民风可太淳朴了。
t_en: Compared to that, the customs of my home feel very unsophisticated.

t_ch: 那里。从那道门走出去，就是卫城。
t_en: There. Once we pass through that gate, we'll be in the acropolis.

t_ch: 终于能看见流窜的士兵了。
t_en: We'll finally see some soldiers roaming about.

t_ch: 尽是些堕入邪道的小卒，干脆利落地击碎它们就是最大的仁慈。
t_en: They're minions who've succumbed to depravity. Smashing them to pieces would be the greatest mercy.

t_ch: 看来阻碍我们的不止有敌人。这桥年久失修，悬锋城的工艺也有不可靠的时候？
t_en: It seems enemies aren't the only things stopping us. This bridge has fallen into disrepair. I suppose even Kremnoan craftsmanship can be unreliable sometimes.

t_ch: 让你失望了，外城是由罪犯建造的。
t_en: Sorry to disappoint, but the outer city was built by convicts.

t_ch: 哎，难怪这么脆弱，没准是这些人在报复你们呢。
t_en: No wonder it's so fragile. Maybe these people were taking their grievances out on you.

t_ch: 无所谓。这里从不以反抗为耻，无论是敌手还是死囚。
t_en: Doesn't matter. There's no shame in resistance here, whether it's from an adversary or a convict on death row.

t_ch: 不过，现在它反而拦住了想杀死{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的人。这种时候，就该怀念缇宝老师的力量了。
t_en: Except now, it's stopping the people trying to kill {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}. It's moments like this that make me miss Tribbie's power.

t_ch: 你没学过祷言吗？
t_en: You never learned the prayer?

t_ch: 「学会」和「精通」是两个概念，没有祭司的共鸣力，普通人念上两句祷言就得昏迷不醒。
t_en: Learning and mastering are two different concepts. Without a priest's resonance, an ordinary person would fall into a coma after chanting the first few words of the prayer.

t_ch: 巧了，我可不是普通人。
t_en: Lucky for you, I'm not an ordinary person.

t_ch: 放着我来！
t_en: Let me do it!

t_ch: 哦？缇宝老师教会你了？
t_en: Oh? Tribbie taught you how?

t_ch: 巧了，这儿有一位专家。
t_en: Lucky for you, I'm an expert.

t_ch: 哦？缇宝老师教会你了？
t_en: Oh? Tribbie taught you how?

t_ch: 别的不说，施展「神迹」可是很看天赋的。没有祭司的共鸣力，普通人念上两句祷言就得昏迷不醒。
t_en: I have to say that the ability to perform miracles is innate. Without a priest's resonance, an ordinary person would fall into a coma after chanting the first few words of the prayer.

t_ch: 既然你自告奋勇，那就麻烦露两手了。
t_en: But since you've volunteered, please be my guest.

t_ch: 看，门开了——
t_en: Look, the gate opened—

t_ch: 天才啊，你不说都没人发现。
t_en: Genius. We never would have known if you hadn't said anything.

t_ch: 走吧，从断桥那儿可以绕路上去。
t_en: Let's go. We can take a detour from the broken bridge.

t_ch: 真是壮观……
t_en: It's magnificent...

t_ch: 你还没见过它最壮观的时候。
t_en: You haven't seen it at its most magnificent.

t_ch: 过去，{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}就是用那柄巨剑摧毁了{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}的天上国度，还有一座又一座城邦。
t_en: That's the giant sword {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} used to destroy {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila's{RUBY_E#} heavenly kingdom long ago, followed by one city-state after another.

t_ch: 它不仅是一柄武器，更是悬锋人的信仰。在战场上英勇牺牲的灵魂，会为{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}手中的锋刃淬火，成为神王伟力的一部分。
t_en: It's not just a weapon, it's the faith of the Kremnoan people. Souls who died heroically on the battlefield will further temper the blade in {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}'s hand, becoming a part of the god-king's power.

t_ch: 这就是泰坦的征伐——{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}、它的眷属、还有信仰「纷争」的人们，所向披靡，战无不胜。即便死去，也将拥抱永恒的荣耀。
t_en: Such is the Titan's conquest — {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}, the Titankin, and the people who have faith in Strife sweep away all their enemies. Even in death, they embrace eternal glory.

t_ch: 有点夸张了吧？悬锋城在黄金战争中的失利，我一下能记起来的就有三场。
t_en: I think you're exaggerating a little. I can think of at least three losses suffered by Castrum Kremnos in the Chrysos War, just off the top of my head.

t_ch: 败给阴谋和毒计并不可耻。应当为此蒙羞的，是那些孱弱的卑劣之徒。
t_en: There's no shame in losing to those who conspire and plot in secret. Those weak and despicable individuals are the ones who should be ashamed.

t_ch: 也对。「宁战死，毋荣归」——悬锋人都是直性子，一生都在奔赴战场，难怪{RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}扎格列斯{RUBY_E#}最爱作弄你们。
t_en: True. Valorous Death Before Glorious Return. The Kremnoan people have always been straightforward. They spend their whole lives running to the battlefield. No wonder {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Zagreus{RUBY_E#} loves teasing you.

t_ch: …但这些也都是过去的事了。
t_en: ...But that's all in the past.

t_ch: 猜猜我在想什么，万敌？
t_en: Guess what I'm thinking, Mydei?

t_ch: 多半不是什么好事。说简单点。
t_en: Probably nothing good. Keep it simple.

t_ch: 这不止是一次远征，也是一场胜负。既然我们来到这儿了…不来酣畅淋漓地比一场吗？
t_en: This isn't just an expedition. It's also a battle to determine a victor. Since we're already here, why don't we have a match and fight to our heart's content?

t_ch: 你和我——重现当初的「悬锋祭典」。
t_en: Just you and me. Let's recreate the original Kremnos Festival.

t_ch: 让我见识见识悬锋城最盛大的搏杀竞技项目，也给眼前这场征伐多镀上一层荣耀的金边。<color=#dbc291ff>看看谁能干掉更多发疯的眷属</color>——如何？
t_en: Show me what the grandest fighting competition in Castrum Kremnos was like. Let's gild this expedition with another layer of glory and <color=#dbc291ff>compete to see who can kill the most crazed Titankin</color>. How about it?

t_ch: 赌注是什么？
t_en: What's the prize?

t_ch: 给{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的最后一击。
t_en: The privilege of delivering the final blow to {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 有点意思。我接受你的挑战。
t_en: Interesting. I accept your challenge.

t_ch: 那我呢？
t_en: What about me?

t_ch: 我成裁判了？
t_en: Am I the referee?

t_ch: 我了解他，这家伙只爱单打独斗。这前面有两条通路，{NICKNAME}就跟我走一边。
t_en: I know Mydei. He prefers to fight alone. There are two paths ahead. {NICKNAME} and I will take the same one.

t_ch: 不竞速，只比战利品的数量，计数全凭自觉。放心，被{F#她}{M#他}解决的敌人，我不会算在自己头上的。
t_en: We won't compete on who's fastest but on the number of trophies, so we'll each be responsible for our own tally. Don't worry. I won't add {M#his}{F#her} kills to my own count.

t_ch: 无所谓，算上又如何？
t_en: Doesn't matter to me even if you do.

t_ch: 你们对这里一无所知，我先让你们十步。
t_en: You don't know the lay of the land, so I'll give you a ten-step head start.

t_ch: 哈哈，完全被看扁了啊。但我可不会谦让——{NICKNAME}，我们出发吧！
t_en: Haha! He already thinks he's won, but I won't decline the offer either. Let's get going, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 还愣着做什么？
t_en: Why are you still standing there?

t_ch: 有来有回，十步太多了——先还你一步。
t_en: Returning the favor. Ten steps are too many, you can have one back.

t_ch: 总算遇到点像样的抵抗了。
t_en: Finally, opponents who look like they can put up a fight.

t_ch: ——上吧，我们要拔得头筹了！
t_en: Let's go! We're going to win this competition!

t_ch: 你和白厄合力打倒了4个泰坦眷属，并从它们身上收集了悬锋徽记……
t_en: You and Phainon defeated 4 Titankins together, and you collected Kremnos crests from their bodies...

t_ch: 打倒了两个，不错的开始。
t_en: That's two down. Not a bad start.

t_ch: 我怎么数都是四个…
t_en: But I counted four...

t_ch: 你数学是不是不太好？
t_en: Not great at math, are you?

t_ch: 那是我俩合力，算到我头上当然要折半，这才叫公平竞争。
t_en: We fought them together, so naturally I can only take half the credit. That's what I call fair play.

t_ch: 为什么要分两路走？
t_en: Why go on separate paths?

t_ch: 分散兵力不会更危险吗？
t_en: Isn't it more dangerous to split up?

t_ch: 别担心，万敌很强，我也一样。
t_en: Don't worry. Mydei is strong, and so am I.

t_ch: 你看见那男人刚才的眼神了吗？他乡愁犯了，不想在旁人面前流露。
t_en: Did you see the look in his eyes? He's wracked by homesickness and doesn't want to show it to anyone else.

t_ch: 毕竟对万敌而言，族人和荣誉大于一切。就算破败成这样，能让他自由自在的地方，也只有悬锋城一处了。
t_en: Nothing is more important to Mydei than his people and glory. The city may be in ruins, but... Kremnos is still the only place where he can be free.

t_ch: 现在我们把他支开，任他想去哪哭鼻子都没所谓。
t_en: Now that we've sent him away, he can go have a good cry wherever he wants.

t_ch: 你们其实关系很好嘛。
t_en: So, you two are actually really close.

t_ch: 亦敌亦友，一直如此。和他一起行动总能激起我的好胜心。
t_en: He's both my friend and my foe. It's always been this way. Working with him always brings out my competitive side.

t_ch: 你真的，我哭死…
t_en: The way you cared... I'm literally in tears...

t_ch: 我们打认识起就是这样，亦敌亦友。和他一起行动总能激起我的好胜心。
t_en: We've been both friends and foes since the day we met. Working with him always brings out my competitive side.

t_ch: 站着不动，是打算投降了？
t_en: Why are you just standing there? Giving up already?

t_ch: 你懂什么，我们是在讨论战略！
t_en: What do you know? We're talking strategy here!

t_ch: 走吧，{NICKNAME}。虽说不是非要比个高下，但我还是很讨厌输的。
t_en: Let's go, {NICKNAME}. The competition might not be the real goal, but I still really hate losing.

t_ch: 咦，你什么时候跑那边去的？
t_en: Huh? When did you get over there?

t_ch: 我早就说过，你们对这城中地形一无所知，如何能赢过我？
t_en: I told you before that you don't know the lay of the land in this city. That's why you won't beat me.

t_ch: 顺便告诉你一声——六个。
t_en: And just so you know: I'm at six.

t_ch: 嚯，看来你运气挺好，我们这条路就有点疏于防卫了。别急，等过了这座铁链桥，我一下就能追回来。
t_en: Wow, seems like luck is on your side. Our path was a little lax on security. Not to worry though, I'll catch up once we cross this iron chain bridge.

t_ch: 前提是你过得去。看见这锁链上的落雷了吗？
t_en: Assuming you can get across. Do you see the lightning bolts striking the chain?

t_ch: 不是一般的落雷啊。看来一股脑地冲过去，跟送死没什么差别。
t_en: It doesn't look like ordinary lightning, which means mindlessly running across would spell certain death.

t_ch: 这里居然有台「预言算碑」…去看看吧，说不定能派上用场。
t_en: There happens to be a Prophecy Tablet here though. Let's go take a look. Maybe it'll help us.

t_ch: 解决落雷的问题前，还是先离那条铁链远点吧。
t_en: Best keep a safe distance from that chain until we find a way to get rid of the lightning.

t_ch: 很好，这样就畅通无阻了。
t_en: Great. Should be smooth sailing from here.

t_ch: 怎么样，看你一脸没办法的样子，我可要先走一步了？
t_en: How's it going? You look stumped. I'll be going on ahead though.

t_ch: 区区闪电，也想拦住悬锋城的战士？
t_en: Mere lightning won't stop a warrior of Castrum Kremnos.

t_ch: 嗯…只是些生活杂物，没什么特别的。
t_en: Hm... Just regular old bits and pieces. Nothing that stands out.

t_ch: 积了一大层灰，应该很久没人动过了。大概是万敌的族人在离开时留下的吧？
t_en: There's a thick layer of dust. Guess no one's been here for a long time. They must have been left behind by Mydei's people when they departed.

t_ch: 他的族人到底是谁？
t_en: Who are his people exactly?

t_ch: 你在奥赫玛一定见过，但只靠双眼很难辨别。
t_en: You've likely seen them in Okhema, but it's hard to tell just by appearance.

t_ch: 很难想象这里曾有人生活…
t_en: It's hard to imagine people used to live here...

t_ch: 这里毕竟是一座战士的城邦，但也有普通的住民。
t_en: It was a city of warriors, but ordinary citizens lived here too.

t_ch: 他们的相貌和他人无异，只是拥有一些…不同于我们的传统。
t_en: They look the same as anyone else. They just have traditions that are slightly... different from ours.

t_ch: 不如再探探吧。跟你在一起的时候我运气总是特别好，万一能碰到什么英雄的遗宝呢？
t_en: Why don't we keep searching? I get especially lucky when I'm with you. Maybe we'll find some hero's lost treasures.

t_ch: 是那种能留声的小玩意，现在的人都不怎么喜欢了。
t_en: It's a gadget that records your voice. People don't like this sort of thing nowadays.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，这个你拿着吧？摇一摇就有声音，作为收集品还蛮有意思的。
t_en: Why don't you hold on to it, {NICKNAME}? It makes a sound when you shake it. Should make for an interesting collectible.

t_ch: 在照片之外，说不定能让你们的旅途更丰富一点？
t_en: Give you something to show for your trip, in addition to the photos.

t_ch: 这是…箴言石板？
t_en: This is... a Proverb Slate?

t_ch: 不像堆放在这里的旧物，看着也太新了，是谁留下的？
t_en: It doesn't look like the rest of the things that were left behind. It looks kind of new, in fact. Who could have left this here?

t_ch: 我也看不懂上面的符文，大概写的是悬锋城的古语。
t_en: I can't read the runes either. It must be written in an ancient language of Castrum Kremnos.

t_ch: 让我也看看。
t_en: Let me have a look.

t_ch: 喏，给你——
t_en: There you go.

t_ch: 该联觉信标起作用了？
t_en: It's time to use the Synesthesia Beacon.

t_ch: 什么意思？行，你来试试吧。
t_en: What does that mean? Alright, give it a go.

t_ch: 我放弃了…
t_en: I give up...

t_ch: 是我高估了自己…
t_en: I overestimated myself...

t_ch: 别怕，隔壁不还有一位专家么？等和万敌合流了，让他看看吧。
t_en: Don't worry. We have an expert next door. We can show it to Mydei when we group back up.

t_ch: 但说起这个…我一直很奇怪，你明明来自天外，为什么能理解翁法罗斯的文字和语言？
t_en: Though that does remind me: I've been wondering how you can understand the language and writing we use in Amphoreus when you're from beyond the sky.

t_ch: 也许翁法罗斯没那么封闭。
t_en: Maybe Amphoreus isn't that isolated after all.

t_ch: 我和丹恒也还没弄清楚。
t_en: Dan Heng and I haven't figured that out yet either.

t_ch: 难道翁法罗斯和天外之界的交集远比人们想象更多？但史料里完全没有这部分记载啊。
t_en: Did Amphoreus have more dealings with worlds beyond the sky than we thought? But nothing like this was ever recorded in our historical materials.

t_ch: 也许，你们的到来…会颠覆很多事吧。
t_en: Maybe your arrival will overturn many things we thought we knew.

t_ch: 先别急着走，{NICKNAME}——我有种直觉，这房间里还藏着什么秘密。
t_en: Wait, {NICKNAME}, don't leave yet. My intuition tells me this room is still hiding a secret.

t_ch: 该翻的地方都翻过了，接着前进吧。
t_en: Well, we've turned over every nook and cranny. It's time to move on.

t_ch: 等等——那家伙打算做什么，想阻拦我们么？
t_en: Wait... What's that creature doing? Is it trying to stop us?

t_ch: …连自己的同伴都要祸害，毫无荣耀可言。
t_en: It even killed its own comrade... They truly have no honor to speak of.

t_ch: 不行，这样行不通啊。
t_en: No, that's not going to work.

t_ch: 对不起，是我太弱小……
t_en: Sorry, it's because I'm too weak...

t_ch: 不是你的问题。悬锋城毕竟是{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的领地，恶劣的环境会阻隔你和{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的共鸣。
t_en: It's not your fault. Castrum Kremnos is {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador's{RUBY_E#} territory, after all. The environment is cutting off your ability to resonate with {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 肯定不是我的问题！
t_en: It's definitely not my fault!

t_ch: 当然不是。悬锋城毕竟是{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的领地，恶劣的环境会阻隔你和{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的共鸣。
t_en: It really isn't. Castrum Kremnos is {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador's{RUBY_E#} territory, after all. The environment is cutting off your ability to resonate with {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 按理来说，这座堡垒中应该有「岁月」祭司专用的仪器…有了，那边。
t_en: In principle, there should be an instrument used by Time Priests somewhere in this fortress... Aha, there it is.

t_ch: 我说什么来着？跟你在一起时运气总是不错。这种仪器能稳固祭司和命运三泰坦的联系，来试试吧。
t_en: What did I tell you? I get especially lucky when I'm with you. This instrument stabilizes the connection between priests and the Three Titans of Fate. Give it a try.

t_ch: 有副作用吗？
t_en: Are there any side effects?

t_ch: 放心吧，如果你出了什么事——「挚友，我一定会把你最后的话带给丹恒！」
t_en: Relax. If something should happen to you, "I'll be sure to pass on your last words to Dan Heng, my friend!"

t_ch: 如果我出了什么事…
t_en: If something should happen to me...

t_ch: 「挚友，我一定会把你最后的话带给丹恒！」
t_en: "I'll be sure to pass on your last words to Dan Heng, my friend!"

t_ch: 别担心，不会有事的。
t_en: Don't worry. You'll be just fine.

t_ch: 果然是中心地带，阵仗不小啊。
t_en: This is the center all right. That's a sizable formation.

t_ch: 数量好多…
t_en: There's a lot of them...

t_ch: 换句话说，我们有机会超过万敌了。
t_en: In other words, this is our chance to overtake Mydei.

t_ch: 这下有机会反超了！
t_en: Now there's a chance to take the lead!

t_ch: 和我想的一样，你也有颗斗士的心啊，朋友。
t_en: My thoughts exactly. You have the heart of a fighter, my friend.

t_ch: 如果记载无误，「天谴之锋」的正下方就是举世闻名的「悬锋斗技场」。我们脚下这条路，或是通向荣耀，或是通向死亡……
t_en: If the records are correct, directly beneath the Blade of Fury lies the world-renowned Kremnos Arena. The path before us leads either to glory or to death...

t_ch: 当然，荣耀是我们的，而死亡——献给它们！
t_en: Of course, glory is ours, and death is theirs!

t_ch: 「凭着强大的实力和无间的默契，两位角斗士击败了所有的敌手……」
t_en: "With their exceptional strength and perfect teamwork, these two gladiators have bested every opponent..."

t_ch: 「…但他们企盼的荣光却没有如期而至。」
t_en: "...but the glory they've been striving for has not come as anticipated."

t_ch: 实在扫兴，连一丝泰坦的气息都没有。{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}是躲起来了？
t_en: How disappointing — no sign of the Titan at all. Is {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} hiding somewhere?

t_ch: 铸魂区，它多半守在那里。
t_en: They're most likely in the Soul-Forging Zone.

t_ch: 通路就在你们身后。
t_en: The path is right behind you.

t_ch: 谢了。那你呢？
t_en: Thanks. What about you?

t_ch: 我走另一条路，那里寄生了很多蛀虫。
t_en: I'll take the other path. It's crawling with parasites.

t_ch: 这场竞赛，你确实占了不少优势啊。{NICKNAME}，我们走？
t_en: You've definitely had the upper hand in this competition. {NICKNAME}, shall we go?

t_ch: 等等，这里真壮观…
t_en: Hold on, this view is incredible...

t_ch: 我想给朋友拍张照片。
t_en: I want to take a picture for my friend.

t_ch: 哦，这番景象可不多见，值得用留影石机记录下来。请随意。
t_en: Well, it's not every day you see a view like this. Go ahead and capture it with the photostone.

t_ch: 她应该对这种景色不感兴趣。
t_en: She is likely not interested in such scenery.

t_ch: 拍就拍。
t_en: I'll take a photo of it then.

t_ch: 风中传来低沉的呢喃。是悠久历史的回响，是神明的挽歌……
t_en: A faint murmur rides the wind — a whisper of ancient history, the elegy of God...

t_ch: …是源自「纷争」的轰鸣。
t_en: ...and the rumble of Strife.

t_ch: 照万敌的说法，我们应该继续深入悬锋城，是那个方向。
t_en: According to Mydei, we need to go deeper into Castrum Kremnos. It's that way.

t_ch: 不过这也有个出口，不知道通往什么地方……
t_en: But there's another exit here. I wonder where it leads.

t_ch: 或许可以用铁球砸碎路障，但愿那边是悬锋城的藏宝室吧，否则可不值当。
t_en: Maybe we can use the iron ball to smash through the roadblock. Hopefully, it leads to Castrum Kremnos' treasure vault. Otherwise, it might not be worth the effort.

t_ch: 楼上也有——也可以想办法把它弄下来。
t_en: There's one upstairs. Maybe we can figure out a way to get it down.

t_ch: 先走哪条路都行，交给你来定夺吧。
t_en: Choose any path you like. It's entirely up to you.

t_ch: 又见面了，现在战况如何？
t_en: We meet again! What's your count so far?

t_ch: 三十。
t_en: Thirty.

t_ch: 那也没差多少，我们还有机会。
t_en: That's not too far off. We still stand a chance.

t_ch: 对了，刚才忘了说，前面拿到一块刻着悬锋城古语的石板，待会儿帮忙翻译下？
t_en: I just remembered — we came across a slate with inscriptions in an ancient language of Castrum Kremnos. Could you help translate it?

t_ch: 超出你知识范围了？无所不知的野史学家。
t_en: Is it beyond your knowledge, amateur historian?

t_ch: 可不是么，只能仰仗你了。
t_en: Sadly, yes. You are my only hope now.

t_ch: 你就继续优哉游哉吧，恕不奉陪。
t_en: You just keep having fun. I've got better things to do.

t_ch: 看起来像是在制造泰坦眷属，或许这就是「铸魂」的意义？
t_en: Looks like they're creating Titankin. Maybe this is what "Soul-Forging" means?

t_ch: 用原石打造出一具躯壳，再注入神明的金血赐予其活力……
t_en: Crafting a body from rough stones and then infusing it with a god's golden blood to bring it to life...

t_ch: 难怪悬锋城能连战连捷，借这种方式，士兵简直源源不绝啊。
t_en: No wonder Castrum Kremnos keeps winning battle after battle. With this method, they can create as many soldiers as they want...

t_ch: 怎么又让你抢先一步…不过这里还剩下不少敌人啊。怎么，累了？
t_en: How did you beat us here again? But there are still plenty of enemies around. What's wrong? Already tired?

t_ch: 留给你们的最后机会，不要也罢。
t_en: I gave you two this final chance on purpose. If you're not interested, fine.

t_ch: 那你还真是慷慨。这里是「堆料区」吧，我们离{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}应该很近了？
t_en: Well, how generous of you. This is the Stockpiling Zone — we must be getting close to {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 没错。也许等你赶到时，它已经是我手里的尸体了。
t_en: That's right. But by the time you arrive, he might already have fallen by my hand.

t_ch: 那是得速战速决，总不能眼睁睁看着你去送死吧？
t_en: Then we must act quickly. We can't let you die right before our eyes, can we?

t_ch: 路断了。奇怪，那他是怎么过去的？难道…是那家伙故意设的路障？
t_en: The path is cut off. But how did he get through? Did... he set up this roadblock on purpose?

t_ch: 真阴险啊，悬锋人！
t_en: Kremnoan people sure are cunning!

t_ch: 别看他表面老实，其实内心精明得很。
t_en: He might look honest, but he's a lot shrewder than he seems.

t_ch: 我觉得你想多了。
t_en: It's all in your head.

t_ch: 别看他表面老实，其实内心精明得很。
t_en: He might look honest, but he's a lot shrewder than he seems.

t_ch: 我跳——
t_en: Let me jump across—

t_ch: 冷静些，咱们可没有不死之身啊。
t_en: Hold your horses! We're not exactly immortal here.

t_ch: 开个玩笑，还是观察下四周吧……
t_en: Enough joking. Let's look around...

t_ch: 这东西也许能帮上忙——<color=#dbc291ff>扎格列斯之手</color>的石台，用它填平断裂的通路就行了。
t_en: This might come in handy — the stone table for the <color=#dbc291ff>Hand of Zagreus</color>. Let's use it to fill the crack in the path.

t_ch: 「扎格列斯之手」？
t_en: The Hand of Zagreus?

t_ch: 简而言之，{RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}扎格列斯{RUBY_E#}是司掌诡计的泰坦，偷盗、欺诈、背叛，这些令人不齿的词语都和它有关。
t_en: Simply put, {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Zagreus{RUBY_E#} is the Titan of trickery — thievery, deception, and betrayal, these unsavory acts are all tied to them.

t_ch: 那位泰坦已经殒落，但在世界各地留下了「蛊惑之手」。人们也会操作这些巨手来进行搬运，还有…咳，偷窃。
t_en: That Titan has fallen, but they left "Bewitching Hands" scattered all over the world. Now, people use these giant hands to move things around — and, well, to steal things, too.

t_ch: 赶路要紧，先不问了。
t_en: Let's get a move on. I'll stop with the questions.

t_ch: 虽然不认可这种行为。但情况特殊，借用一次吧。
t_en: I don't normally approve of this, but given the circumstances, let's use it this one time.

t_ch: 哎…又来？
t_en: Ugh, not again.

t_ch: 这次也许得用它把障碍吹走。
t_en: This time, we might be able to use it to blow away the barrier.

t_ch: 这样就好了吧？但愿别再被莫名其妙的机关挡住去路了。
t_en: This should work now. Let's just hope no more strange mechanisms get in our way.

t_ch: 悬锋人用那双如鹰隼般锐利的双眼追踪着你，你很确定他可以坚持一天一夜不眨眼。
t_en: The Kremnoan follows you with his eagle-sharp gaze. You're sure he can keep his eyes open day and night without blinking.

t_ch: 太慢了。
t_en: You took your sweet time.

t_ch: 抱歉，谁叫有人没把路上的障碍清扫干净呢？
t_en: Apologies, but someone forgot to clear out the obstacles along the way.

t_ch: 废话少说，看看你们的结果吧。
t_en: Enough chatter. Show me your tally.

t_ch: 万敌展示出他收集的三十枚悬锋城徽记。
t_en: Mydei displays the thirty Castrum Kremnos crests in his collection.

t_ch: 与万敌比较了徽记总数……
t_en: Compare the total number of crests you obtained with Mydei's...

t_ch: 哈！看来赢家是我们啊。
t_en: Aha! Looks like we won!

t_ch: 愿赌服输，最后一击给你了。
t_en: Well, I'm a man of my word. You can deliver the final blow.

t_ch: 等等，我又想了想——这赌注还是太大了些。靠一场比试决定荣耀的归属，不是敬神的表现。
t_en: Wait... On second thought, the stakes seem a bit too high. Deciding who gets the glory with just one competition might be disrespectful to the gods.

t_ch: 我修改赌注，你只要把收获的战利品分我们一半就行。至于{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}…我们会联手将其击败。
t_en: Let's change the stakes. You can just share half of your spoils with us. As for {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}, we can take them down together.

t_ch: …前提是你我做得到。
t_en: ...The real question is if we can pull it off.

t_ch: 你要的战利品，拿去吧。
t_en: Well, here's your spoils.

t_ch: …看来确实技不如人。
t_en: ...Looks like I could work on my skills a bit more.

t_ch: 愿赌服输，给{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的最后一击让给你吧。
t_en: Well, I'm a man of my word. You can deliver the final blow to {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 如此轻易就让出了最后一剑的荣光，你也配得上战士之名么？
t_en: Giving away the glory of delivering the final blow that easily? I thought you were a real warrior.

t_ch: 不愧是悬锋城的「王储」，对这些繁文缛节还真是重视啊。
t_en: I guess being the crown prince of Kremnos makes you obsessed with all this ceremonial stuff.

t_ch: 靠一场比试决定荣耀的归属，不是敬神的表现。你我胜负未分，继续吧——
t_en: Deciding who gets the glory with just one competition might be disrespectful to the gods. Let us declare no victor and continue on—

t_ch: {RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}，它的亡魂属于真正的强者。
t_en: {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}'s soul should be claimed by true warriors.

t_ch: 你这家伙，有时候说话还挺好听的。
t_en: Surprisingly, your words can sometimes be music to my ears.

t_ch: 平手，真是凑巧啊。
t_en: It's a tie. Funny how things work out, huh?

t_ch: 既然如此，给{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的夺命一剑就由我们一起刺出，你说呢？
t_en: In that case, how about we deliver the final blow to {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} together? What do you say?

t_ch: 既然胜负未分，那就继续吧——
t_en: Since there is no victor, let's continue on—

t_ch: {RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}，它的亡魂属于真正的强者。
t_en: {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}'s soul should be claimed by true warriors.

t_ch: 哈哈——就这么决定了，我们平分这份荣光。
t_en: Haha, it's settled then. Let's divide the glory equally.

t_ch: 所以，它就在前面了？
t_en: They're right up ahead, then?

t_ch: 气息微弱，但它就在这里。我们与「纷争」的路途，即将交汇。
t_en: There's just a faint scent, but I'm certain they're here. We're about to cross paths with Strife.

t_ch: 这个大家伙，莫非是真言狮口？
t_en: Wow, look at this big guy. Could this be Verax Leo?

t_ch: 放尊重些。这是「黄金狮首」，别和那些满口流言野史的东西相提并论。
t_en: Show some respect. This is Chryseus Leo, not some rumor-spouting gossip-monger.

t_ch: 黄金狮首是悬锋城首屈一指的参谋，它的谏言为我族赢下了诸多战役，化解了无数阴谋。
t_en: Chryseus Leo was the top advisor in Castrum Kremnos, whose wise counsel helped my tribe win many battles and thwart numerous schemes.

t_ch: 遗憾，如今它已失语。黄金裔若能借用它的智慧——
t_en: Sadly, he has lost his voice now. If only the Chrysos Heirs could benefit from his wisdom—

t_ch: 迈德…漠斯？
t_en: M—Mydeimos...

t_ch: 王…储……
t_en: Crown... prince?

t_ch: 什么动静？！
t_en: What's that sound!?

t_ch: 说话了，它说话了！
t_en: He... He speaks!

t_ch: …狮首？
t_en: ...Leo?

t_ch: 已经过去许多年了…你还醒着？
t_en: After all these years, you're still awake?

t_ch: 迈德…漠斯……
t_en: M—Mydeimos...

t_ch: 我不会…忘记您的声音。我一直…在等待这个时刻。
t_en: I could never... forget your voice. I've been waiting for this moment... all along.

t_ch: 说吧，吾师！我会聆听你的教诲。
t_en: Please, teacher, share your wise counsel with me!

t_ch: 王储啊…悬锋城已经失去了一切。它的人民…传统…以及最为宝贵之物，荣光。
t_en: Crown prince... Castrum Kremnos has lost everything — its people, traditions... and most importantly, its glory.

t_ch: 人民为生存迁徙，传统被时光消磨，但荣光？哪怕隐入浓雾，城墙崩析，悬锋城从未背弃战士的荣耀。为何口出此言？
t_en: To survive, our people have migrated, and our traditions have faded with time. But glory? Even though it has vanished into the mist and its walls have crumbled, Castrum Kremnos has never forsaken the glory of its warriors. Why say otherwise?

t_ch: 我们的神明…已经彻底失去理智。它不再是万人敬畏的战争图腾…它已然堕落，正在酝酿着毫无荣光的阴谋……
t_en: Our god has completely lost their sanity. No longer a revered symbol of war, they have fallen and are now plotting an evil scheme...

t_ch: 我恳求你，迈德漠斯…终结它漫长的痛苦，让它以战士的身份死去！它必须…带着荣耀……
t_en: I urge you, Mydeimos, to put an end to their endless suffering. Allow them to die as a warrior! They must... go with honor...

t_ch: 吾师？
t_en: Teacher?

t_ch: 吾师——回应我！我以王储的名义，命令你……
t_en: Teacher, answer me! As your crown prince, I command you—

t_ch: …万敌。
t_en: ...Mydei.

t_ch: 让它休息吧，它应该已经累了。
t_en: Let him rest. He must be exhausted.

t_ch: …也许只是一头老狮子临终前的谵语，不必放在心上。我们继续赶路……
t_en: ...Perhaps those were just the delirious words of a dying lion. I shouldn't overthink it. Let's keep going.

t_ch: 等等，这是先前提到的石板，只凭我们无法解读。
t_en: Hold on. This is the slate that I mentioned earlier. We couldn't decipher it.

t_ch: 说不定和那头老狮子口中之事有关。
t_en: Maybe it's connected to the scheme the old lion mentioned.

t_ch: 给我。
t_en: Hand it to me.

t_ch: 不…{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}绝对不会……
t_en: No... {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} would never...

t_ch: 难怪奥赫玛会遭遇袭击，没想到堕入疯狂的泰坦还能有此算计……
t_en: That explains the surprise attack on Okhema. I didn't think that the mad Titan could have orchestrated it...

t_ch: 奥赫玛将要面临灭顶之灾。必须有人回援。
t_en: Okhema is on the brink of destruction. Someone needs to go back and rescue them.

t_ch: 不、不对。我们要接着前进，完成我们的使命。
t_en: N—No, we need to press on and finish our mission.

t_ch: 你被功名戳瞎双眼了吗？我早就说过，若想摘得胜利，唯有全军出动。
t_en: Are you blinded by glory? As I said before, victory only lies in assembling a full-fledged army.

t_ch: 我们距离{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}只有一步之遥，只要战胜了恶念的源头，阴谋自会消散。
t_en: We're just one step away from {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}. Defeat the source of the evil, and the threat will vanish on its own.

t_ch: 就如同那可怕的一击，你和我也是离弦的箭矢。{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}能给奥赫玛带来灭顶之灾，反过来，我们也一样。
t_en: Just like that decisive strike, there is no turning back for us. {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} could drive Okhema to the brink of ruin, but we can do the same to them.

t_ch: 而且…我信任阿格莱雅和缇宝老师。奥赫玛有两位半神驻守，还有我们的伙伴，她们能保护好圣城。
t_en: Besides, I have faith in Aglaea and Tribbie. With two demigods and our allies watching over Okhema, they should be more than capable of protecting the holy city.

t_ch: 丹恒也在。
t_en: Dan Heng is there too.

t_ch: 没理由前功尽弃。
t_en: We can't let our efforts go to waste.

t_ch: …好，相信一次你们的判断。但愿我们不会被推进悔恨的深渊。
t_en: ...Alright, I'll trust your judgment this time. Let's hope we don't end up in an abyss of regrets.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}…你的神躯果然被疯狂之手掏空了么？你已彻底舍弃战士的尊严了么……
t_en: {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}... Has your divine body truly been hollowed out by the hands of madness? Have you completely forsaken your dignity as a warrior?

t_ch: 温度又升高了，这里还在运作吗？
t_en: It's getting even hotter. Is this place still in use?

t_ch: 穿过这里，就是铸魂区。
t_en: After we pass through here, we'll reach the Soul-Forging Zone.

t_ch: 燥热不堪。杂念也在脑海里滋生……
t_en: The heat is unbearable, and all these thoughts are echoing in my head...

t_ch: 现在想想，我们的确有可能战死在这里，对吗？
t_en: On second thought, this could very well be where we meet our end, right?

t_ch: 小心，你的思绪正在被「纷争」腐蚀。
t_en: Be careful. Your thoughts are being corrupted by Strife.

t_ch: 你说的没错，继续深入是我的坚持。而且{NICKNAME}也在，我们一定会完成使命，并全身而退。
t_en: You're right. It was my decision to keep pushing forward. And with {NICKNAME} here, we're sure to complete our mission and return safely.

t_ch: 就像眼前的道路，尽管残破不堪，但一定会通向终点。
t_en: Just like the path ahead, even though it's completely dilapidated, it will still lead us to our final destination.

t_ch: 伟哉，吾主{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: All hail {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}, my master and guide.

t_ch: 壮哉，战争之魂不朽。
t_en: Immortal soul of war, ever by my side.

t_ch: 眷属，我问你——悬锋城是否正准备灭绝奥赫玛？
t_en: Titankin, let me ask you this: Is Castrum Kremnos planning to destroy Okhema?

t_ch: 你的沟通技巧还真直白…
t_en: You don't beat around the bush, do you?

t_ch: 你有什么妙语？我洗耳恭听。
t_en: What do you propose? I'm all ears.

t_ch: 这家伙的级别够吗？
t_en: This might be above its pay grade.

t_ch: 没有别的选择了。
t_en: There aren't any other options.

t_ch: 伟哉，吾主{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: All hail {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}, my master and guide.

t_ch: 壮哉，战争之魂不朽。
t_en: Immortal soul of war, ever by my side.

t_ch: 的确尚有理智，可惜剩得不多。
t_en: It is indeed rational, but just barely.

t_ch: 「战争之魂不朽」……
t_en: "Immortal soul of war"...

t_ch: 走吧。或许附近还有能交流的眷属。
t_en: Let's keep going. There might be other Titankin nearby we can talk to.

t_ch: 不把它「处理」掉吗？
t_en: Aren't you going to "take care" of it?

t_ch: 你若是视作威胁，请便——我没有出手的理由。
t_en: If you see it as a threat, go ahead. I have no reason to do it.

t_ch: 我来击碎它——
t_en: Let me smash it—

t_ch: 慢着！别在这里白费力气，还是用点巧劲吧。
t_en: Hold your horses! Don't waste your energy here — use your head.

t_ch: 看来又得拜托它了。
t_en: Looks like we'll need to use the hand again.

t_ch: 以永不锈蚀之钢，雕琢永不磨灭之身。
t_en: Sculpt an indestructible body with steel that never rusts.

t_ch: 伟哉，{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}…君临万邦。壮哉，{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}…举世畏栗。
t_en: The all-powerful {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}, ruler of all lands. The all-divine {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}, bringer of a thousand fears.

t_ch: 「永不磨灭之身」……
t_en: "An indestructible body"...

t_ch: 进攻奥赫玛的{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}只是一具半身。按它的意思，悬锋城中不会还有吧？
t_en: The {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} that attacked Okhema was only a double. Considering what the Titankin said, could there be more of them in Castrum Kremnos?

t_ch: 大战之末，{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}莫名陷入疯狂，神力不断流失。如今…那位泰坦残存的力量不及过去的十分之一。
t_en: By the end of the war, {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} had succumbed to madness, and their divine power dwindled. Now, the Titan's remaining strength is less than a tenth of what it once was.

t_ch: 为自己打造半身的确能稳固势力。但相比光荣的死亡，它竟选择了用此种方式苟延残喘…令人唏嘘。
t_en: Creating doubles for themself might have stabilized their power, but instead of a glorious death, they chose to drag out their miserable existence. How tragic.

t_ch: 看来它已经彻底失去了战士的尊严。你带领族人离开是正确的决定，万敌。
t_en: They seem to have forfeited all their dignity as a warrior. Leading your people away from here was the right choice, Mydei.

t_ch: 我的抉择无需粉饰，救世主。不过是一次放下骄傲的自我流放，不值得被编撰为颂歌。
t_en: There's no need to glorify my choice, Deliverer. It was just a self-imposed exile that cost me my pride. Not something worth celebrating in a song.

t_ch: 但若它所言属实，对阵{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}注定是一场苦战。
t_en: But if the Titankin's words are true, going up against {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#} is destined to be a bitter fight.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>不过说到不死之身，你也是这个领域的专家啊。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Speaking of indestructible bodies, you're quite the expert in that area yourself.</color>

t_ch: 废话少说。
t_en: You talk too much.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，还没和你提起过吧？黄金裔们可是各个身怀绝技。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, did I ever tell you? The Chrysos Heirs are all endowed with unique powers.

t_ch: 而传言中，悬锋城那位战无不胜的王储，万敌——他的天赐就是「拒绝死亡」。
t_en: Legend has it that Mydei, the invincible crown prince of Kremnos, has a talent for "defying death."

t_ch: 英灵，为{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}天谴之矛{RUBY_E#}献上汝之战魂……
t_en: Martyrs, consecrate your souls of war for the {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Lance of Fury{RUBY_E#}...

t_ch: 成为{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的利刃…贯穿敌人的心脏与机核……
t_en: You shall become {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}'s blade... to pierce through the enemy's heart and core...

t_ch: 成为供喂火种的柴薪…将腐烂的世界付之一炬……
t_en: You shall be the timber to nourish the Coreflame... to reduce the decayed world to ashes...

t_ch: 「付之一炬」？就靠阴谋诡计？
t_en: "Reduce the decayed world to ashes"? With nefarious schemes?

t_ch: 它们和悬锋城一样，被那疯狂扭曲了。虽然仍记得如何杀戮，但忘却了杀戮背后的意义。
t_en: They've been twisted by madness, just like Castrum Kremnos. They remember how to kill, but they've forgotten why.

t_ch: 但我一向觉得，用刀杀人，不比用计谋杀人更高贵。
t_en: I've always believed that killing with blades isn't any nobler than killing with subterfuge.

t_ch: 除了使命和生存，没有其它动因能为暴力辩护。
t_en: Aside from one's mission and survival, no other reason can justify violence.

t_ch: 这便是你我之间的歧异。于我族眼中，荣光和征服亦是正当的理由。
t_en: That's where we differ. For my tribe, glory and conquest are also valid reasons.

t_ch: 但你并非打心底里认同这些，不是么？
t_en: But deep down, you don't really agree with that, do you?

t_ch: 英灵……
t_en: Martyrs...

t_ch: 嗯？这家伙…刚才是面对着我们的吗？
t_en: Hmm? Was it facing us the whole time?

t_ch: 我也走神了…
t_en: I wasn't paying attention either...

t_ch: 说话慢吞吞的，可爱捏。
t_en: Isn't it kind of cute how slowly it speaks?

t_ch: …拿起武器！
t_en: ...Ready your arms!

t_ch: 为{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}天谴之矛{RUBY_E#}……
t_en: For the {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Lance of Fury{RUBY_E#}...

t_ch: 献上汝之战魂！
t_en: Consecrate your souls of war!

t_ch: 我已经放弃理解这些家伙的行为逻辑了。简直就是一片混乱。
t_en: I've given up on figuring out the logic of these minions. They're just pure chaos.

t_ch: 无妨，你我很快就要给这场疯狂画上句点了。
t_en: No matter. You and I will soon bring this madness to a close.

t_ch: 做好准备了吗，救世主？
t_en: Are you ready, Deliverer?

t_ch: 时时刻刻都准备着。背负「纷争」的火种…那是我的夙愿。
t_en: Always. Bearing the Coreflame of Strife... has been my deep-seated desire.

t_ch: 我期许的命运…就在这扇门后。
t_en: The fate I've been hoping for... lies behind this door.

t_ch: 无用的杂念，无数人因它在战场上丢掉性命。
t_en: Useless distractions. Countless lives have been lost on the battlefield because of it.

t_ch: 别忘了，我们会站在此处是因为你的坚持。那就拿出那该死的觉悟。
t_en: Remember, you insisted that we come here. Now, show us that cursed resolve!

t_ch: 我该怎么假装这只是一场普通的战斗？
t_en: But how can I pretend that this is just an ordinary battle?

t_ch: 我的使命、意义…全都押在这里。你难道忘得了吗？族人的苦难…被侵蚀的故土……
t_en: My mission and purpose... all depend on this. Can you forget the suffering of your people and your corroded homeland?

t_ch: 当然不会忘记。但我掌握了一种技巧，你或许无法理解。
t_en: Never! But I've learned a method you probably wouldn't understand.

t_ch: 所有的悔恨、愤怒，在这种时刻，我学会了掌控它们，将它们淬炼成一把锋利无比的兵器，为我所用……
t_en: All the anger and regret I feel right now, I've learned to control them and hone them into a razor-sharp weapon for my use...

t_ch: 它有个简单的名字：杀意。
t_en: It has a simple name: Intent to kill.

t_ch: 你真是一头野兽。
t_en: You really are a beast.

t_ch: 但现在，也许我们都该屈从内心的兽性。
t_en: But right now, perhaps it's best for us to submit to the beasts inside.

t_ch: 腐朽的神！直面我，迎接你的末日吧！
t_en: Corrupted god! Face me and meet your doom!

t_ch: 我是悬锋之子，神谕中的黄金裔。我为你带来了最公平的价码——
t_en: I am a son of Kremnos and a Chrysos Heir of the prophecy, and I now offer you the fairest deal—

t_ch: 以我的一千道伤疤和一百条性命，换你在史诗中荣耀的死亡！
t_en: I trade a thousand scars and a hundred lives of mine for your glorious death!

t_ch: 战魂正在悬锋城上空聚集…正在涌向「天谴之锋」！
t_en: The souls of war are amassing in the skies above Kremnos... surging toward the Blade of Fury!

t_ch: 它根本没想决出胜负，只是在利用一次又一次死亡，为手中的锋刃淬火…哪怕经受百死，也要贯穿{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}的躯体！
t_en: They have no intention of winning the fight. Instead, they're using death after death to temper their blade... Even with a hundred deaths, they've resolved to pierce through {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}'s body!

t_ch: 毫无荣誉之心…我族的神，你偏偏就堕落到这种地步？
t_en: All their honor is gone... How did the god of our tribe fall so low?

t_ch: 救世主，给你个当英雄的机会。
t_en: Deliverer, here is your moment.

t_ch: 把这片战场交给我。我会拖住这具躯壳，让它无暇挥舞「天谴之锋」。带着消息回去…通知那两位半神。
t_en: I'll hold them off here and keep them from wielding the Blade of Fury. You need to head back and notify the two demigods.

t_ch: 你疯了吗，凭一人之力对抗泰坦？那我也要留下。{NICKNAME}，拜托你——
t_en: Are you out of your mind, facing the Titan alone? I'm staying too. {NICKNAME}, please—

t_ch: 赶紧滚！非得让我这么说？
t_en: Get lost! Do you have to make me say this?

t_ch: 我必须留下，不是为了奥赫玛的安危，而是要和自己的神明做个了结。
t_en: I must stay here, not for Okhema's safety, but to settle the score with my god.

t_ch: 就算不死是种诅咒，它也不该被运用得如此卑劣。
t_en: Even if immortality is a curse, it shouldn't be used so despicably.

t_ch: 走吧，你的战场另有他处，「救世主」。
t_en: Go, Deliverer. Your battlefield lies elsewhere.

t_ch: …坚持住，别死太多次了。
t_en: ...Stay strong. Try not to get killed too many times.

t_ch: 终于没那么聒噪了。
t_en: Finally, some peace.

t_ch: 你和我——两个被死亡拒之门外的战士，世间还有比这更加公平的竞技吗？
t_en: You and I — two warriors rejected by death. Is there any contest more fair than this?

t_ch: 来！未能成王之王，已失神格之神！我们多么登对，就在这里——
t_en: Come forth! The king who has never claimed his throne, against the god who has fallen from grace. How perfectly matched we are!

t_ch: 厮杀到万物殆尽吧！
t_en: Let's battle till the world's final breath!

t_ch: 故地重游，感觉如何？
t_en: How does it feel to be home again after so long?

t_ch: 不是这里，我住在内城。
t_en: Not here. I lived in the inner city.

t_ch: 呵…不愧是<color=#dbc291ff>悬锋的王储</color>。
t_en: Oh... As befitting of <color=#dbc291ff>the crown prince of Kremnos</color>.

t_ch: 厉害！真娴熟啊。
t_en: Amazing! You do have the touch!

t_ch: 又断了…看来泰坦的力量也维持不了这桥的稳定。
t_en: And it's broken again... I guess even a Titan's power isn't enough to stabilize this bridge.

t_ch: 就像罪犯的灵魂一样，脆弱不堪。
t_en: Just like the soul of a criminal: Fragile.

t_ch: 看，天上！那就是……
t_en: Look, in the sky! That's...

t_ch: 「天谴之锋」——即便世界已经破碎，它依然高悬于世人头顶。
t_en: The Blade of Fury — Even if the world lay shattered, it would still hang high above everyone's heads.

t_ch: 顺利着陆！希望这条路上的敌人对得起咱俩的火力。
t_en: Made it! Hopefully the enemies on this side are worthy of our combined might.

t_ch: 喔…真是壮观。
t_en: Whoa... That's impressive.

t_ch: 沿着铁索走到对面，通往那柄巨剑的道路，就该有这种气魄。
t_en: Following an iron chain, onto the path leading to the giant sword on the other side? This is more like it!

t_ch: 哎哟，动作这么利索？
t_en: Whoa! You're nimbler than I thought!

t_ch: 看好你自己吧，这底下可是无尽深渊。
t_en: Worry about yourself. There's an endless abyss below us.

t_ch: ——这才刺激！
t_en: —That's more like it!

t_ch: 不止命多，而且命大——我都快妒忌你了。
t_en: You practically have nine lives and, on top of that, you're lucky? I'm almost jealous.

t_ch: 这一关过得真险…幸好我俩平衡感都还不错。
t_en: Whew... That was a close one. Thankfully we both have a remarkable sense of balance.

t_ch: 这里是休息室？有些格格不入啊。
t_en: So, this is a lounge? It feels out of place.

t_ch: 过去参与竞技的战士，都是在这儿休整的么？
t_en: Maybe this is where all the warriors in the past took their breaks during competitions.

t_ch: 前进的路被铁球挡住了……
t_en: The road ahead is blocked by the iron ball...

t_ch: 能再呼唤一次{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}奇迹吗？
t_en: Can you call upon {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}'s Miracle again?

t_ch: 不错，感觉你的状态都不一样了。
t_en: Not bad. Something feels different about you already.

t_ch: 现在再尝试一次？
t_en: Why don't you give it another go?

t_ch: 就是这样，做得不错。
t_en: That's it! Nice work.

t_ch: 这动静…咱们再不加把劲，肯定要输给他了。
t_en: From the sound of things, we're about to lose if we don't step it up.

t_ch: 小心，距离「天谴之锋」已经很近了……
t_en: Careful. We're getting very close to the Blade of Fury...

t_ch: 「迎面走来的是令人闻风丧胆的角斗士组合，黑暗剑士白厄——」
t_en: "Approaching now are the duo of gladiators whose names strike terror in all who hear them — the one and only Dark Swordmaster, Phainon!"

t_ch: 「——还有银河球棒侠！」
t_en: "And the Galactic Baseballer!"

t_ch: 「残忍的一击！球棒侠没有给{M#他}{F#她}的敌人留下生路！听到了吗？排山倒海的喝彩声——」
t_en: "What a savage hit! The Baseballer shows no mercy to {M#his}{F#her} enemies. Can you hear that? The crowd's roaring in approval!"

t_ch: 「嗯…稍微需要些想象力。」
t_en: "Hmm... This requires a bit of imagination."

t_ch: 「漂亮的配合！这两位角斗士——」
t_en: "What marvelous teamwork! These two gladiators..."

t_ch: 你们俩的妄想症吵到我了！
t_en: These delusions of yours are seriously getting on my nerves!

t_ch: 「…还要面对场外的噪音干扰，真可怜啊。」
t_en: "...must also contend with the noise from outside the arena. How unfortunate!"

t_ch: 大功告成，继续前进。
t_en: There, all done! Let's move on.

t_ch: 此路不通？难不倒我们。
t_en: Blocked path? That won't stop us!

t_ch: 怎么楼梯口也被堵住了，这是人住的地方吗？
t_en: Why's the staircase blocked? Weird to think that people lived here.

t_ch: 漂亮！去收获我们应得的奖励吧，反正悬锋城是没人用得上了。
t_en: Well done! Go claim our rewards — no one else in Kremnos will have any use for them anyway.

t_ch: 堕落的泰坦，失心的眷属，荒凉的堡垒……
t_en: A fallen Titan, soulless kin, and a desolate fortress...

t_ch: 记忆和现实的矛盾，在各地都成为常态了啊。
t_en: This place has fallen pretty far from the way people remember it.

t_ch: 前面又有一幅壁画。凑近些看看？
t_en: There's another mural up ahead. Want to take a closer look?

t_ch: 马上就要跟万敌汇合了，<color=#dbc291ff>要不要把这片区域清理干净，多收集些</color>{RUBY_B#<color=#dbc291ff>悬锋徽记</color>}<color=#dbc291ff>战利品</color>{RUBY_E#}<color=#dbc291ff>？</color>
t_en: We'll be meeting up with Mydei soon. <color=#dbc291ff>Want to clean up this area and increase our</color> {RUBY_B#<color=#dbc291ff>Kremnos crest</color>}<color=#dbc291ff>trophy</color>{RUBY_E#} <color=#dbc291ff>count?</color>

t_ch: 成败在此一举，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Victory or defeat, it all comes down to this, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 现在，想办法把断裂的路面铺平。
t_en: We need to find a solution to mend the broken path.

t_ch: 天衣无缝，做得真不错。
t_en: Flawless work. Great job!

t_ch: 这么多石料，不知能打造多少战争机器？
t_en: So many stones here. I wonder how many war machines can be built with all this?

t_ch: 但今天过后，它们就只能永远沉睡了。
t_en: But after today, they'll be slumbering here forever.

t_ch: 看，有人快等得不耐烦了。
t_en: Look, someone's getting impatient.

t_ch: 闷热。泰坦的怒火点燃了空气。
t_en: The sweltering heat — the Titan's wrath has set the air ablaze.

t_ch: 嗯，我们很接近了……
t_en: Yeah, we're getting pretty close—

t_ch: 小心——有敌人。
t_en: Look out! An enemy!

t_ch: 收起你的剑…它似乎尚有理智。
t_en: Put your sword away... It seems to still be rational.

t_ch: 也许我们能得到些蛛丝马迹。
t_en: We might be able to get some clues from it.

t_ch: 你的残酷不亚于悬锋城的刽子手。
t_en: Your ruthlessness is akin to that of the executioners in Castrum Kremnos.

t_ch: 翻译一下…好吧，我也拿不准这句的意思。
t_en: Let me translate that for you... Actually, I'm not sure what it means either.

t_ch: 我们与一位泰坦的殒落如此接近…这感觉很虚幻。
t_en: We're so close to the fall of a Titan... It feels unreal.

t_ch: 不必再将它视作神明，你，我，它——只是身陷死斗的战士，仅此而已。
t_en: Don't think of them as a god. It's just you, me, and them — warriors in a fight to the death.

t_ch: 那时的压迫感…又回来了。你感受到了吗，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: That oppressive feeling from earlier — it's back. Do you feel it, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 但那股气焰不再纯粹，它掺杂着血腥味，还有亡灵的哭嚎……
t_en: But that bluster is no longer pure. It's mingled with the scent of blood and the cries of lost souls...

t_ch: 万敌，我做不到和你一样……
t_en: Mydei, I may not be able to fight like you...

t_ch: 但我会以自己的方式战斗，狩猎神明！
t_en: But I will do battle in my own way and bring down this god!

t_ch: 别想再用劣质的半身应付我们——粉碎吧！
t_en: Stop hiding behind those pathetic doubles! We'll crush you!

t_ch: 终于愿意现出真身了吗，泰坦？
t_en: Finally ready to reveal your true self, Titan?

t_ch: 堕落的神，尝尝人的怒火吧！
t_en: Feel the wrath of humanity, corrupted god!

t_ch: 它已是强弩之末了！
t_en: They're already near their end!

t_ch: 碾碎「纷争」的战甲，摘下它的火种！
t_en: Shatter the armor of Strife and strip away the Coreflame!

t_ch: 你与白厄在掩护下撤离、回到奥赫玛……
t_en: You and Phainon retreat using the opportunity and return to Okhema...

t_ch: 万敌与尼卡多利的战吼自颓垣之后传出…不要辜负了万敌的搏命鏖战，抓紧时间破解尼卡多利的秘密吧。
t_en: Mydei and Nikador's battle cries can be heard from beyond the crumbling walls... Don't let Mydei's desperate struggle be in vain. Hurry up and uncover Nikador's secrets as fast as you can.

t_ch: 此处已被断壁残垣封锁，你们曾与尼卡多利鏖战的那片战场，如今已随慑人的战吼一同成为追忆。
t_en: This place has been sealed off by the crumbled walls. The battlefield where you once faced Nikador has now become nothing more than a memory alongside the terrifying howls of battle.

t_ch: 你们那位朋友…是叫「三月」，对吧？
t_en: So, that friend of yours... her name is "March"?

t_ch: 对，她叫三月七。
t_en: That's right, she's called March 7th.

t_ch: 「三月七」…亏你还觉得我们的名字奇怪。
t_en: March 7th... and you think our names sound strange?

t_ch: 之前在创世涡心，听你们提起过她。
t_en: You guys mentioned her in the Vortex of Genesis earlier.

t_ch: 我们和你提起过她吗？
t_en: Did we mention her to you before?

t_ch: 之前在创世涡心，听你们提起过她。
t_en: You guys mentioned her in the Vortex of Genesis earlier.

t_ch: 其实我一直想说，论名字的奇特程度，这位也不遑多让吧？
t_en: I must say, her name is pretty unconventional, wouldn't you agree?

t_ch: 你们一定很珍视和她的关系，才会时刻帮她记录旅行中的点滴。
t_en: You must really value your friendship with her to go through all this effort to document the journey for her.

t_ch: 她是非常重要的伙伴。
t_en: She is an important friend.

t_ch: 嗯，我能感觉到。
t_en: Yeah, I can tell.

t_ch: 她本来也要来的，但身体抱恙。
t_en: She was supposed to be here, but she's not feeling well.

t_ch: 啊…那还真遗憾。
t_en: Ah, that's unfortunate.

t_ch: 实不相瞒，正是两位对待朋友的态度，让我对你们多信任了几分。
t_en: Honestly, seeing how you both look out for your friend has furthered my trust in you.

t_ch: 一个人道德的山尖，往往就是他对待朋友的方式。老师教给我的东西，大多都还回去了，但这一句依旧清晰。
t_en: A person's true character often comes to light in their friendships. Most of what my teacher taught me has slipped away, but this sentence is still vivid in my memory.

t_ch: 尤其在这动荡的时代，值得托付后背的同伴…弥足珍贵。
t_en: Especially in these tumultuous times, friends who you can trust completely are a rare and precious find.

t_ch: 接着赶路吧，不能让那家伙太得意。那扇紧闭的大门，也许可以用预言算碑打开。
t_en: Let's keep going. We can't let Mydeimos get too full of himself. The Prophecy Tablet might be able to unlock that closed gate.

t_ch: 我们回来了。
t_en: We're back.

t_ch: 呜…缇安，好累。感觉好晕…小敌他，会安全吗？
t_en: Hnngh... Tired... and dizzy... Will De be okay?

t_ch: 没事的…{RUBY_B#万敌}迈德漠斯{RUBY_E#}是我见过最强悍的战士。
t_en: He'll be fine... {RUBY_B#Mydei}Mydeimos{RUBY_E#} is the fiercest warrior I know.

t_ch: 「不死之身」是怎么回事？
t_en: What's this indestructible body about?
